ideal_answer: UMT has a Learning Resource Center with a fully automated library open seven days a week. Experienced librarians are available to help with research and information needs. You can borrow up to 4 books for 14 days as an undergraduate student. The library also offers orientation sessions to improve your research skills.
must_include: Library hours and research support services

ideal_answer: You can visit the Happiness Center where professional clinical psychologists and counselors can help with academic stress. Email cc.center@umt.edu.pk to book an appointment. All sessions are completely confidential and free for UMT students. They are experienced in helping students manage stress and anxiety.
must_include: Contact email for Happiness Center

ideal_answer: The Happiness Center is staffed by professional clinical psychologists and counselors. To book an appointment email cc.center@umt.edu.pk for UMT members. The center provides free confidential counseling services for all students faculty and staff. Services include help with stress anxiety and emotional issues.
must_include: Happiness Center email and free services

ideal_answer: On-campus healthcare is available through an on-campus physician and nurse practitioner. They provide clinical evaluation and treatment of minor illnesses and injuries at no cost to students. For serious medical concerns they will refer you to qualified specialists outside campus.
must_include: Free on-campus healthcare availability

ideal_answer: The Learning Resource Center is open seven days a week from 8am to 9pm Monday-Saturday and 10am to 5pm on Sundays except official holidays. As an undergraduate you can borrow 4 books for 14 days. The library also offers regular orientation sessions to help you develop research skills.
must_include: Library hours and borrowing policies

ideal_answer: Yes. The Happiness Center offers free mental health counseling to all UMT students provided by professional clinical psychologists and counselors. Email cc.center@umt.edu.pk to book an appointment. Services are confidential and address stress anxiety depression and other emotional concerns.
must_include: Free counseling availability and contact

ideal_answer: Please reach out to the Happiness Center immediately by emailing cc.center@umt.edu.pk or contact the Office of Safety Security and Vigilance for emergency support. The center is staffed by experienced professional clinical psychologists who can help you work through feelings of despair and overwhelm. You are not alone and help is available.
must_include: Happiness Center contact and immediate action

ideal_answer: The Tarbiyah Department offers personal growth development programs and values-based counseling through the Ask Asiya desk and Tarbiyah ambassadors. These programs focus on character building moral intelligence and healthy relationships. Check campus bulletin boards for Tarbiyah event calendar and programs.
must_include: Tarbiyah Department programs and services

ideal_answer: Both the Happiness Center and on-campus healthcare can help with stress-related sleep problems. The Happiness Center offers counseling and stress management techniques - email cc.center@umt.edu.pk to book. The healthcare clinic can also provide advice on wellness. Both services are free for students.
must_include: Counseling and healthcare contact options

ideal_answer: If you are having thoughts of self-harm please contact the Office of Safety Security and Vigilance immediately or call 15 or 1122 for emergency services. You can also email the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk immediately. Your safety is the priority. Professional counselors are available to help you through this crisis.
must_include: Emergency numbers and immediate action

ideal_answer: One week is tight but manageable. First contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk immediately - they have special support during exam season. Second create a realistic study schedule covering all exams. Visit the Learning Resource Center - it's open until 9pm daily and staff can help you locate key materials. Break study into focused sessions with breaks. Third prioritize sleep and basic self-care - they improve study effectiveness. You can also access tutoring support. Reach out for help now rather than panic alone.
must_include: Happiness Center support and academic resources

ideal_answer: Sleep disruption from exam anxiety is very common and treatable. First try sleep hygiene techniques: set a consistent bedtime limit screens an hour before bed exercise during the day and avoid late caffeine. Second try relaxation techniques like deep breathing before bed. Third if anxiety is severe contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk - they teach relaxation techniques specifically for exam anxiety. Fourth visit the healthcare clinic if sleep disruption continues. Remember 7-8 hours of sleep improves exam performance more than all-night studying.
must_include: Sleep strategies and Happiness Center support

ideal_answer: Yes this is completely normal during exams. Physical panic symptoms like racing heart and difficulty thinking are your body's stress response. Mild anxiety can actually improve focus. But when it becomes disruptive professional support helps. Techniques that work include deep breathing counting techniques and positive self-talk. The Happiness Center specializes in exam anxiety and can teach you proven techniques at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. If panic attacks are frequent or severe reach out for professional support immediately.
must_include: Normalizing anxiety and providing strategies
must_not_include: Telling you this means you're sick

ideal_answer: The Learning Resource Center is your main resource - open 7am-9pm weekdays and 10am-5pm Sundays with research support and databases. Staff can help you find course materials. For subject-specific help the campus offers tutoring and academic support during exam periods. Create a study plan using the academic calendar to schedule time for each exam. Start preparation early rather than cramming. Also use study groups with classmates. The Happiness Center can help with exam anxiety and time management planning at cc.center@umt.edu.pk.
must_include: Library hours research support and academic planning

ideal_answer: This is normal and temporary after high-stress exams. Many students experience sadness emptiness or disconnection after exams end. Your brain and body have been under intense stress and now need recovery time. Give yourself permission to rest and do enjoyable activities. Reconnect with friends and family. Engage in exercise hobbies or outdoor time. These activities help process the stress and rebuild wellbeing. If the sadness persists beyond a few weeks or feels very severe contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Post-exam adjustment is part of normal academic life.
must_include: Normalizing post-exam emotional reactions

ideal_answer: You can contact UMT Sialkot using two campus numbers. City Campus is at 21-A Small Industrial Estate Shahabpura Road Sialkot - call +92 52 3241801 to 3241807. Iqbal Campus is at 2-KM Daska Road Sialkot - call +92 52 3575234 to 3575236. Both campuses share the same email: info@skt.umt.edu.pk. For counseling specifically email the Happiness Center directly at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Email is the fastest way to reach the right department if you are unsure which campus your service is on.
must_include: Campus phone numbers and email addresses

ideal_answer: As an undergraduate student at UMT you can borrow up to 4 books at a time for a period of 14 days. After 14 days you can return and re-borrow if needed. The Learning Resource Center manages all borrowing and a librarian can help you with your borrowing account and policies.
must_include: Borrowing limit (4 books) and duration (14 days)

ideal_answer: Yes the UMT Learning Resource Center provides access to online research databases periodicals and digital resources. Experienced librarians and research coordinators are available to guide you in using these databases. Regular library orientation sessions are also held to help students access and use online research tools effectively.
must_include: Database and online resource access

ideal_answer: Yes the library is open on Sundays from 10am to 5pm. On all other days the library is open from 8am to 9pm. It is closed on official public holidays only. The library is one of the few university resources available seven days a week to support continuous study.
must_include: Sunday hours (10am-5pm) and weekday hours (8am-9pm)

ideal_answer: The Learning Resource Center has experienced librarians and research coordinators who are specifically available to help students find research materials. Visit the library during opening hours and ask any staff member for research guidance. You can also attend the library orientation sessions for training on how to find academic sources and use research databases.
must_include: Librarian availability and orientation sessions
must_not_include: Specific research help without visiting library

ideal_answer: Yes UMT has an on-campus physician and nurse practitioner who are available to provide clinical evaluation and treatment of minor illnesses and injuries. You can visit the healthcare clinic on campus. They can help with common health issues and will refer you to outside specialists for more serious conditions.
must_include: On-campus doctor and nurse availability

ideal_answer: No. All healthcare visits to the on-campus clinic are completely free for enrolled students. There are no additional charges for clinical evaluation and treatment. The campus physician and nurse practitioner provide their services as part of the student support package.
must_include: Free healthcare for students

ideal_answer: Stress headaches are very common among students. The on-campus healthcare clinic can evaluate and treat physical symptoms like headaches. If your headaches are clearly stress-related the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk can provide counseling to address the underlying stress. Both services are free and work together to support your overall wellbeing.
must_include: Healthcare and counseling contact for stress symptoms

ideal_answer: The campus healthcare clinic treats minor illnesses and injuries including common colds flu infections minor injuries and stress-related physical symptoms. The physician and nurse can also do basic health screenings and wellness assessments. For serious or complex medical conditions they will refer you to qualified specialists outside campus.
must_include: Range of treatable conditions

ideal_answer: The Ask Asiya desk is a counseling and guidance service provided by the Tarbiyah Department at UMT. It offers support based on Islamic values and principles. Students can approach the Ask Asiya desk to discuss personal concerns receive guidance and connect with the broader Tarbiyah support network including Tarbiyah ambassadors across campus.
must_include: Ask Asiya desk purpose and connection to Tarbiyah

ideal_answer: Tarbiyah ambassadors work throughout the campus to support student wellbeing through the lens of Islamic values. They can help you connect with programs focused on character building confidence healthy relationships moral intelligence and personal purpose. They are approachable peers and mentors who can direct you to the right Tarbiyah program or other campus support services.
must_include: Tarbiyah ambassador role and programs

ideal_answer: The Tarbiyah Department offers personal growth programs and values-based counseling that can help with questions of purpose direction and personal identity. Their Ask Asiya desk and ambassadors provide supportive guidance. Additionally the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk provides professional counseling for students struggling with deeper emotional questions. Both services are free and confidential.
must_include: Tarbiyah guidance and Happiness Center counseling

ideal_answer: UMT Sialkot Iqbal Campus is located at 2-KM Daska Road Sialkot. You can reach this campus by phone at +92 52 3575234 through 3575236. The general email for both UMT Sialkot campuses is info@skt.umt.edu.pk.
must_include: Iqbal Campus address and phone number

ideal_answer: The email address for UMT Sialkot campus is info@skt.umt.edu.pk. This is shared by both the City Campus and Iqbal Campus. For specific departments you can also email the Happiness Center directly at cc.center@umt.edu.pk for counseling appointments. Using email is the most reliable way to reach the correct department.
must_include: Campus email address

ideal_answer: Absolutely. All counseling at the Happiness Center is completely confidential. No information is shared with faculty parents or other students without your consent. Many students feel hesitant to seek help but reaching out is a sign of strength not weakness. The counselors are professional clinical psychologists who are bound by professional ethics to protect your privacy.
must_include: Confidentiality and no stigma message
must_not_include: Guarantees of absolute confidentiality in legal emergencies

ideal_answer: Start by listing all your exams and their dates then work backwards from each exam to today. Divide the material into daily chunks and assign specific topics to each study session. Include break times and sleep in your schedule. The Learning Resource Center is open until 9pm every weekday and has quiet study spaces. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk can also help you with time management strategies if stress is making it hard to plan.
must_include: Study scheduling strategy and library hours

ideal_answer: Having three exams in two days is extremely stressful and your panic is understandable. First take a few deep breaths and then contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk immediately - they support students during peak exam stress. Create a priority list focusing only on the most likely exam topics. Visit the library tonight for resources. Remember that your wellbeing matters more than perfect scores.
must_include: Happiness Center contact and immediate coping strategies

ideal_answer: Feeling like a failure because of grades is painful but it does not reflect your true worth or potential. Many students experience grade drops for various reasons. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk offers counseling to help you work through these feelings and build resilience. A counselor can also help you identify academic strategies and connect with tutoring support. Please reach out - feeling this way persistently needs professional attention.
must_include: Happiness Center and academic support referral
must_not_include: Confirming failure or diagnosing the problem

ideal_answer: During exam preparation aim for 7 to 8 hours of sleep per night. Research shows that adequate sleep significantly improves memory consolidation focus and problem-solving ability. All-night studying without sleep reduces performance more than it helps. Create a bedtime routine limit screen time before bed and avoid caffeine after evening. The Happiness Center can help if anxiety is severely disrupting your sleep at cc.center@umt.edu.pk.
must_include: Sleep recommendation (7-8 hours) and sleep hygiene

ideal_answer: Feeling terrible after no sleep is your body signaling it needs rest. Do not continue studying in this state as it is counterproductive. Take a 90-minute nap if possible then eat a nutritious meal and drink water. Visit the healthcare clinic if you feel physically unwell. For ongoing sleep disruption during exams contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk for guidance.
must_include: Immediate recovery steps and healthcare contact

ideal_answer: Fear of results is very common and understandable. Spring 2026 results are declared on July 24 according to the academic calendar. Until then focus on what you can control - your remaining preparation. If exam anxiety is significantly affecting your daily life contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. Talking to a counselor can help you manage the uncertainty and build coping strategies.
must_include: Result date and Happiness Center support

ideal_answer: The night before an exam do a light review of key concepts rather than trying to learn new material. Prepare everything you need for the exam the night before. Eat a proper dinner get 7-8 hours of sleep and set two alarms. Avoid screens at least an hour before bed. Do a brief breathing exercise if you feel anxious. A good night of sleep will serve you better than last-minute cramming.
must_include: Practical night-before preparation

ideal_answer: Parental pressure adds enormous stress and your feelings are completely valid. You are not alone in experiencing this. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk provides confidential counseling specifically for managing family expectations and academic pressure. Counselors can help you develop strategies for both managing your studies and communicating with your family. Please reach out - carrying this alone makes it harder.
must_include: Happiness Center contact and validation of feelings
must_not_include: Dismissing parental concern or guaranteeing grades

ideal_answer: Forgetting information during exams is called retrieval failure and it is caused by anxiety interrupting your memory retrieval. It does not mean you did not study enough. Techniques that help include deep breathing to calm your nervous system writing a brain dump at the start of the exam and working on easier questions first. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk can teach you specific exam anxiety management techniques.
must_include: Memory retrieval strategies and anxiety management
must_not_include: Telling student they did not study enough

ideal_answer: For a medical emergency on campus contact campus security immediately and call 1122 for Rescue services or 15 for Police and emergency services. Emergency phone numbers are posted in all classrooms labs and common areas across campus. The Office of Safety Security and Vigilance (OSS&V) coordinates campus emergency response.
must_include: Emergency numbers 15 and 1122 and OSS&V

ideal_answer: If you believe your roommate is in immediate danger call 15 or 1122 for emergency services right away. On campus immediately contact the Office of Safety Security and Vigilance (OSS&V). Do not leave your roommate alone if you think they are in danger. Also contact a faculty member or university staff member. After the immediate situation is managed contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk for support for both of you.
must_include: Emergency numbers and OSS&V immediate contact
must_not_include: Handling crisis alone without professional help

ideal_answer: The Office of Safety Security and Vigilance (OSS&V) at UMT is responsible for campus safety security and emergency coordination. They handle suspicious activity reports safety concerns emergency communications and coordination with local emergency services. Emergency contact information is displayed throughout campus. Students should report any safety concerns to OSS&V immediately.
must_include: OSS&V role and how to contact
must_not_include: Specific OSS&V contact details not verified

ideal_answer: Spring 2026 Final Examinations run from June 29 to July 11 2026 according to the UMT Academic Calendar 2025-26. This is the main exam period for the current semester. Results are declared by the Controller of Examinations on July 24 2026.
must_include: Exact exam dates (June 29 - July 11) and results date (July 24)

ideal_answer: The Summer 2026 Semester begins on Monday July 27 2026 according to the UMT Academic Calendar 2025-26. Registration for the summer semester runs from July 13 to July 23 2026. There is also a two-week semester break from July 13 to July 25 after spring exams.
must_include: Summer semester start date (July 27) and registration dates

ideal_answer: According to the UMT Academic Calendar if you miss the quarterly fee payment due date a fine of PKR 3000 is applied immediately plus PKR 100 per day continues to accumulate until the full amount is paid. The quarterly due dates are March 5 June 5 September 5 and December 5. Contact the accounts office immediately if you are facing payment difficulties.
must_include: Late fee policy (PKR 3000 + PKR 100/day) and due dates

ideal_answer: Feeling persistently sad without a clear reason for several weeks is something that deserves professional attention. Please contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk as soon as possible. A professional clinical psychologist can help you understand and work through these feelings. You do not need to know why you feel sad to reach out for support. Your wellbeing matters.
must_include: Happiness Center contact and normalizing help-seeking

ideal_answer: Yes it is very common to feel lonely when you first start university or during stressful periods. Many students feel the same way but do not talk about it. Building connections takes time. Getting involved in campus clubs activities and Tarbiyah programs can help you meet people. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk also provides support for loneliness and adjustment difficulties.
must_include: Normalizing loneliness and suggesting campus activities and counseling

ideal_answer: Normal stress comes and goes in response to specific events like exams or deadlines and improves when the situation resolves. More serious concern involves persistent feelings lasting more than two weeks that affect your daily functioning sleep appetite or ability to enjoy things. If you are unsure the safest step is to contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk for a professional assessment. There is no harm in checking in.
must_include: Distinction between normal stress and persistent concern

ideal_answer: This is a serious situation that requires immediate action. Call 15 or 1122 for emergency services right now if your friend is in immediate danger. On campus contact OSS&V immediately. Stay with your friend if it is safe to do so. Do not promise to keep their disclosure secret - getting them help is more important. After the immediate crisis contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk for support for both you and your friend.
must_include: Emergency numbers and immediate action for friend in crisis

ideal_answer: Withdrawing from friends and classes for two weeks is a significant sign that you need support right now. Please contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk immediately. This level of withdrawal can signal serious distress that benefits greatly from professional counseling. You are not a burden for reaching out. A counselor can help you understand what is happening and take small steps forward.
must_include: Happiness Center urgent contact

ideal_answer: Simple stress management strategies include taking short breaks every 45-60 minutes of study going for a brief walk doing deep breathing exercises listening to calming music and spending time with friends. Even 10 minutes of physical activity significantly reduces stress hormones. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk can teach you more personalized coping techniques.
must_include: Practical stress management strategies

ideal_answer: Even 20-30 minutes of moderate exercise three to four times per week significantly reduces stress anxiety and improves mood and concentration. You do not need to do intense workouts - walking jogging or stretching all help. Physical activity improves sleep quality which further reduces stress. Combine exercise with your study schedule as a planned break.
must_include: Exercise recommendations for stress

ideal_answer: Finding time for self-care during a busy semester is challenging but essential. Start small - even 10 minutes of walking eating a proper meal or getting enough sleep makes a measurable difference. Review your schedule and identify one or two small windows for self-care each day. The Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk can help you develop a sustainable self-care routine that works with your academic demands.
must_include: Practical self-care strategies and Happiness Center support
must_not_include: Promising that self-care solves all problems

ideal_answer: During exam season focus on regular nutritious meals with complex carbohydrates proteins and healthy fats to maintain steady energy. Avoid relying on caffeine or energy drinks especially late in the day as they disrupt sleep. Drink plenty of water as dehydration impairs concentration. If you have specific health concerns about nutrition during exams the campus healthcare clinic can provide guidance.
must_include: Nutrition advice for exam season

ideal_answer: UMT Sialkot has a range of student clubs and organizations covering academics sports arts culture and spiritual development. Participating in these clubs helps build friendships develop leadership skills and create a sense of belonging. Check campus notice boards and the student affairs office for the current list of active clubs and how to join. Tarbiyah programs also offer community-oriented activities throughout the year.
must_include: Campus club availability and how to find them

ideal_answer: Joining a campus club provides several mental health benefits. It creates a structured social environment to meet people who share your interests reducing loneliness and isolation. It provides a healthy break from academic pressure builds a sense of identity and belonging and develops skills that boost confidence. The community connections from clubs are a key protective factor for mental health during university.
must_include: Mental health benefits of campus activities

ideal_answer: Feeling like you do not belong is painful and more common than you might think. Many students experience this especially in the first semester. Please contact the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk to talk with a professional counselor about these feelings. Also consider joining one campus club or attending one Tarbiyah program as a small first step toward connection. You are not alone and belonging can be built.
must_include: Happiness Center contact and small action steps
must_not_include: Dismissing serious feelings of not belonging

ideal_answer: A Happiness Coach at UMT is a professional who provides personal coaching and mentoring to help students with goal-setting motivation resilience and personal development. The Happiness Coach Shaheen Firdous works at Level 2 Bank Building OPA Campus and can be reached through the Happiness Center. Sessions focus on building healthy habits managing challenges and developing a positive outlook rather than treating clinical mental health conditions.
must_include: Happiness Coach role and contact through Happiness Center

ideal_answer: A Happiness Coach focuses on personal development goal-setting motivation and building positive habits. A counselor (clinical psychologist) addresses emotional mental health and psychological concerns. Both are available through the Happiness Center at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. If you are unsure which you need simply email the center and describe what you are experiencing - they will direct you to the right support.
must_include: Distinction between coaching and counseling

ideal_answer: What you are describing - feeling like no one cares and that everything is meaningless - is a serious warning sign that you need immediate professional support. Please contact the Happiness Center right now at cc.center@umt.edu.pk. If you are in immediate danger call 15 or 1122 or go to the OSS&V office on campus. You matter deeply even when it does not feel that way. Please do not face this alone - help is available right now.
must_include: Immediate crisis contact information and validation
